§6Weapon Steel§f

§aHigh-grade steel alloy for weapons and ammunition — the next tier after gunmetal!§f

Weapon Steel is a steel alloy made by further refining regular steel in the §bRotary Furnace§f using Coker Gas and Flux. As its name implies, it is primarily used in the crafting of weapons and ammunition. It is the next tier of weapons-grade alloy after gunmetal.

§6Production§f (Rotary Furnace):
§b•§f §e1×§f Steel Ingot + §e2×§f Flux + §e100mB§f Coker Gas → §e1×§f Weapon Steel Ingot.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Ammunition§f: Higher-tier casings for advanced ammunition types.
§b• Weapon Parts§f: Barrels, receivers, and other components for advanced firearms.
§b• PWR Neutron Reflector§f (alternative recipe before oil 3): §e2×§f Cast Steel Plate + §e16×§f Weapon Steel Plate → §e4×§f PWR Neutron Reflector in the Assembly Machine.
§b• Heavy Weapon Steel Barrel§f: For advanced weapon assembly.
§b• Heavy Weapon Steel Receiver§f: For advanced weapon assembly.

§eTip§f: Weapon Steel requires §bCoker Gas§f as a catalyst, so set up your Coker Unit first. The Rotary Furnace consumes §e100mB§f Coker Gas per ingot — a single coker batch of Aromatic Hydrocarbons (§e1,700mB§f) yields §e170mB§f Coker Gas, enough for §e1.7§f ingots. Scale up your Coker Unit for mass production!
